cards.back: A statistical method used to compare means of three or more groups to determine if at least one group mean is different from the others
The null hypothesis states that all group means are equal, while the alternative hypothesis states that at least one group mean is different from the others
A type of ANOVA used to compare means of three or more groups where only one independent variable is being tested
A type of ANOVA used to compare means of three or more groups where two independent variables are being tested
An experimental design used in ANOVA where each treatment is assigned to a specific position in a square grid, ensuring all treatments are equally represented
